title: elymus shandongensis
intro: Elymus shandongensis, also known as Chinese wild rye, is a type of perennial grass native to China. It has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving cough and sore throat, and improving digestion. In herbal preparations, Elymus shandongensis is often combined with other medicinal plants and used in the form of decoctions, infusions, or dried powder.
